Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title, duration and popularity of each Track of which its popularity is a at least 10 and at most 20.
Answer: SELECT        track_title,        duration,        popularity FROM        Track WHERE        popularity >= 10        AND popularity <= 20;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the year of the Album titled 'American Pie'
Answer: SELECT year FROM Album WHERE Album_title =  'American Pie';

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT        country   FROM        Market   WHERE        album_id = (           SELECT                album_id           FROM                Album           WHERE                album_title = 'American Pie'       );

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT        Track_title   FROM        Track   WHERE        Track.track_id IN (           SELECT                track_id           FROM                Writes           WHERE                role = 'lyricist'       );

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT album_title   FROM Album   WHERE NOT EXISTS (   SELECT *   FROM Market   WHERE    Market.album_id = Album.album_id               AND Market.country = 'DE'       );

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT        year,        COUNT(*) AS num_albums   FROM        Album   GROUP BY        year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT        role,        COUNT(*) AS role_count   FROM        Writes   WHERE        NOT role = 'unknown'   GROUP BY        role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT         Artist.name,         SUM(Track.duration) AS total_duration,         AVG(Track.popularity) AS average_popularity FROM     Artist, Track, Performs WHERE     Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id     AND Track.track_id = Performs.track_id GROUP BY     Artist.name ORDER BY     total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A WHERE A.artist_id IN (   SELECT artist_id    FROM Performs   WHERE artist_id IN (SELECT artist_id FROM Produces)     AND artist_id IN (SELECT artist_id FROM Writes) );

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.name FROM Artist A WHERE EXISTS (   SELECT *   FROM Performs P, Produces PR, Writes W   WHERE P.artist_id = A.artist_id     AND PR.artist_id = A.artist_id     AND W.artist_id = A.artist_id     AND P.track_id = PR.track_id     AND PR.track_id = W.track_id );

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND P.track_id = W.track_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id AND P.track_id = PR.track_id;

Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT * FROM Track T, Performs P, Produces D, Writes W WHERE (A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id = T.track_id) AND (A.artist_id = D.artist_id AND D.track_id = T.track_id) AND (A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id) );

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Track T, Performs P, Produces D, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND A.artist_id = D.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND P.track_id = T.track_id AND D.track_id = T.track_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id;

Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A WHERE A.artist_id IN (   SELECT P.artist_id FROM Performs P )  AND A.artist_id IN (   SELECT Pr.artist_id FROM Produces Pr ) AND A.artist_id IN (   SELECT W.artist_id FROM Writes W );

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A WHERE EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM Performs P     WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id     AND P.track_id IN (         SELECT track_id FROM Produces WHERE artist_id = A.artist_id     )     AND P.track_id IN (         SELECT track_id FROM Writes WHERE artist_id = A.artist_id     ) );

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT A.name, SUM(T.duration), AVG(T.popularity) FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.track_id = T.track_id GROUP BY A.name ORDER BY SUM(T.duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces Pr ON A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces Pr ON A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND P.track_id = Pr.track_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND P.track_id = W.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title, duration and popularity of each Track of which its popularity is a at least 10 and at most 20.

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT Market.country  FROM Market, Album  WHERE Album.album_id=Market.album_id  AND Album.album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Track.track_title  FROM Track, Artist, Writes  WHERE Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id  AND Writes.role = 'lyricist'  AND Track.track_id=Writes.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Album.album_title  FROM Album  WHERE Album.album_id  NOT IN (SELECT Market.album_id FROM Market WHERE Market.country = 'DE');

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT year, COUNT(*) FROM Album GROUP BY year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT role, COUNT(*) FROM Writes WHERE NOT role IS NULL GROUP BY role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration), AVG(Track.popularity)  FROM Artist, Track, Performs  WHERE Performs.artist_id=Artist.artist_id AND Performs.track_id=Track.track_id  GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY SUM(Track.duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.* FROM Artist A, Performs P, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id=P.artist_id  AND  A.artist_id=Pr.artist_id  AND A.artist_id=W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.* FROM Artist A, Performs P, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id=P.artist_id  AND  A.artist_id=Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id=P.artist_id AND P.track_id=Pr.track_id AND P.track_id=W.track_id AND A.artist_id=W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, AVG(Track.popularity), SUM(Track.duration) FROM Track, Performs, Artist WHERE Track.track_id = Performs.track_id AND Artist.artist_id=Performs.artist_id GROUP BY Artist.name, Track.popularity, Track.duration ORDER BY Track.popularity DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T, Writes W, Produces Pr WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.artist_id=W.artist_id AND W.artist_id=Pr.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T, Writes W, Produces Pr WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.artist_id=W.artist_id AND W.artist_id=Pr.artist_id AND W.track_id=P.track_id AND Pr.track_id=W.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration), AVG(Track.popularity) FROM Artist, Track, Performs WHERE Performs.artist_id = Artist.Artist_id AND Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY SUM(Track.duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Produces PR, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND A.artist_id = PR.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration) AS total_duration, AVG(Track.popularity) AS average_popularity FROM Artist JOIN Performs ON Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id JOIN Track ON Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs Pe, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = Pe.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT country FROM Album, Market WHERE Album.album_id=Market.album_id AND album_title='American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Track.track_title FROM Track JOIN Writes ON Track.track_id = Writes.track_id JOIN Artist ON Writes.artist_id = Artist.artist_id WHERE Writes.role = 'lyricist';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT album_title FROM Album WHERE album_id NOT IN (SELECT album_id FROM Market WHERE country = 'DE');

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT year, COUNT(*)  FROM Album GROUP BY year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT role, COUNT(*) FROM Writes WHERE role IS NOT NULL GROUP BY role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration) AS total_duration, AVG(Track.popularity) FROM Artist JOIN Performs ON Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id JOIN Track ON Performs.track_id = Track.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id JOIN Track T ON P.track_id = T.track_id AND PR.track_id = T.track_id AND W.track_id = T.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT name, SUM(duration) AS total_duration, AVG(popularity) FROM Track T, Artist A, Performs P WHERE T.track_id = P.track_id AND A.artist_id = P.artist_id GROUP BY name ORDER BY total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND P.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND P.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id=T.track_id;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces PR ON A.artist_id = PR.artist_id AND P.track_id = PR.track_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND P.track_id = W.track_id;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P,Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND Pr.track_id = W.track_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND P.track_id = Pr.track_id AND Pr.track_id = W.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT      a.name AS artist_name,      SUM(t.duration) AS total_duration,      AVG(t.popularity) AS avg_popularity FROM      Artist a JOIN      Performs p ON a.artist_id = p.artist_id JOIN      Track t ON p.track_id = t.track_id GROUP BY      a.name ORDER BY      total_duration DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Produces Pr, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A JOIN Performs P ON A.artist_id = P.artist_id JOIN Produces Pr ON A.artist_id = Pr.artist_id JOIN Writes W ON A.artist_id = W.artist_id WHERE P.track_id = Pr.track_id AND Pr.track_id = W.track_id;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT name FROM Artist, Performs, Produces, Writes WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the year of the Album titled 'American Pie'
Answer: SELECT Album.year FROM Album WHERE Album.album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Albums in Spotify are available for different markets. Give the SQL query that returns the country for each Market in which the Album 'American Pie' is available.
Answer: SELECT Market.country FROM Market, Album WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id AND Album.album_title = 'American Pie';

Q Text: Give the SQL query that returns the title of each Track of which the Artist that Writes (wrote) the Track had the role 'lyricist' (So return tracks with lyricists).
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Track.track_title FROM Track, Artist, Writes  WHERE Writes.role = 'lyricist'  AND Writes.artist_id = Artist.artist_id AND Writes.track_id = Track.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the title of each Album that is NOT available in the country 'DE'.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Album.album_title  FROM Album, Market  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM Market WHERE Album.album_id = Market.album_id AND Market.country = 'DE');

Q Text: Give the SQL query that return for each year the number of albums in that year.
Answer: SELECT Album.year, COUNT (*)  FROM Album GROUP BY Album.year;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists for each Writer's role, the total number of times that this role occurs in the database. Do not list the number of time that the role is unknown.
Answer: SELECT Writes.role, COUNT(*) FROM Writes WHERE Writes.role NOT NULL GROUP BY Writes.role;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT Artist.name, SUM(Track.duration), AVG(Track.popularity) FROM Track, Artist, Performs WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Track.track_id = Performs.track_id GROUP BY Artist.name ORDER BY SUM(Track.duration) DESC LIMIT 5;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Artist.artist_id, Artist.name  FROM Artist, Performs, Writes, Produces WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT Artist.artist_id, Artist.name  FROM Artist, Performs, Writes, Produces, Track WHERE Artist.artist_id = Performs.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Writes.artist_id AND Artist.artist_id = Produces.artist_id AND Track.track_id = Produces.track_id AND Performs.track_id = Track.track_id;

Q Text: Give the SQL query that lists the name of each Artist, the total duration of the tracks they perform in and the average popularity those tracks . Order the results by the total duration (longest first) and return only the top 5 artists.
Answer: SELECT a.name, SUM(t.duration), AVG(t.popularity) FROM Artist a, Track t, Performs p WHERE a.artist_id = p.artist_id AND t.track_id = p.track_id GROUP BY a.name ORDER BY AVG(t.popularity) DESC LIMIT 5 ;

Q Text: An application on the music database returns each Artist that Performs, Writes, and Produces at least on Track (possibly different tracks) with the following SQL query: SELECT A.artist_id, A.nameFROM Artist AWHERE EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Performs P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Produces P, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id  AND P.track_id=T.track_id)AND EXISTS (  SELECT *  FROM Writes W, Track T  WHERE A.artist_id = W.artist_id  AND W.track_id=T.track_id); Simplify the query by removing subqueries and by removing unnecessary comparisons. Use DISTINCT if needed.
Answer: SELECT DISTINCT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A, Performs P, Track T, Produces P2, Writes W WHERE A.artist_id = p.artist_id AND P.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = P2.artist_id AND P2.track_id=T.track_id AND A.artist_id = W.artist_id AND W.track_id=T.track_id ;

Q Text: Give an SQL query that returns each Artist for which there is a Track that the artist Performs, Writes, and Produce. So, similar to the previous question, but the artist should be performer, producer and writer of the same track...
Answer: SELECT A.artist_id, A.name FROM Artist A WHERE EXISTS (   SELECT *   FROM Performs P, Track T, Produces P2, Writes W   WHERE A.artist_id = P.artist_id   AND P.track_id=T.track_id   AND P.track_id=P2.track_id   AND P.track_id=W.track_id   AND P.artist_id=P2.artist_id   AND p.artist_id=W.artist_id );
